seoDescription: Compare climate risk scores between Hectorville and Henley Beach. See which suburb is safer from flood, bushfire, heat, and storm hazards.
ogTitle: Hectorville vs Henley Beach: Climate Safety
ogDescription: Data-driven comparison of climate risk in Hectorville and Henley Beach.
heading: Hectorville vs Henley Beach — Which is Safer?
intro: Compare climate risk between Hectorville, South Australia and Henley Beach, South Australia. Our analysis uses data from BOM, CSIRO, and state hazard authorities to score each suburb across 6 climate hazards.
verdict: Henley Beach has a lower overall climate risk score (35/100) compared to Hectorville (40/100). We recommend getting an address-level report for properties in both suburbs before making a purchase decision.

faq.question: Is Hectorville or Henley Beach safer from flooding?
faq.answer: Hectorville has a flood risk score of 50/100, while Henley Beach scores 37/100. A lower score indicates lower flood risk. Get an address-level flood risk report for a specific property.

faq.question: Which suburb has lower bushfire risk?
faq.answer: Hectorville scores 57/100 for bushfire risk. Henley Beach scores 21/100. Fire risk depends on proximity to vegetation and local fire history.

faq.question: Should I buy in Hectorville or Henley Beach?
faq.answer: Both suburbs have climate considerations. Henley Beach has a lower overall climate risk score. We recommend getting a full ClimateNest report for any specific property you're considering, which includes 8 hazard scores, insurance projections, and actionable recommendations.

valueImpact.description: Properties in this area may face a 1.52% climate value drag over time due to rising insurance costs and climate adaptation expenses.
